title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power dynamics for two millennia have been shaped by a divide between continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The lecture frames Putin and Xi's actions through this geographical and historical lens.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine traces two millennia of global power dynamics to a structural divide: continental empires such as China and Russia pursue security through territorial conquest, while maritime powers such as Britain and the United States pursue prosperity through trade. She applies this geographical and historical lens to the actions of Putin and Xi, framing them as expressions of a continental security doctrine rather than isolated choices. The central takeaway is that geography and the contrast between land-based and sea-based strategies continue to shape contemporary geopolitical competition.
